name: Family Sports Center Ice Arena
price_public_skate: $8.50 district / $11 non-district (M-F days) | $8/$10 (Fri eve & weekends) | Children under 3 free | Check ssprd.org/Family-Sports-Ice-Schedule
price_stick_puck: Take-A-Shot: District $11 / Non-district $13 (M-F) | Drop-in Hockey 18+: $12/$13.50 | Reserve by calling (303) 708-9500
spectator_notes: Avalanche Grill: Full restaurant & bar on-site with views of the ice. Full menu, beer & wine. Open during all sessions.
notes: 150,000 sq ft entertainment complex. Colorado Avalanche practice facility - sometimes fans can watch. Two NHL sheets. Also has mini golf, laser tag, climbing wall, bumper cars, arcade. Part of South Suburban Parks and Recreation District (SSPRD). | On-site shop: Family Sports Pro Shop

name: The Broadmoor World Arena
price_public_skate: $6 admission | $4 skate rental | Check broadmoorworldarena.com for current schedule
price_stick_puck: Pick-up hockey limited to 20 skaters + 3 goalies. Must be 18+. Sign up online (no phone reservations as of Jan 2026).
notes: 8,000-seat main arena plus adjacent Ice Hall with two practice rinks: one NHL-size, one Olympic-size. Home to Colorado College Tigers. For Learn to Skate, Learn to Play Hockey, or Public Skate info, call Ice Hall Office at (719) 477-2178. | On-site shop: World Arena Pro Shop

name: Colorado College - Ed Robson Arena
address: 14 E Cache La Poudre St, Colorado Springs, CO 80903
price_public_skate: $10 adults / $5 kids
notes: 3,400-seat arena opened Sept 2021. First on-campus home for CC Tigers in 83 years. 120,000+ sq ft. Public skating open to community. Transforms to 4,600-seat venue for concerts. Part of Colorado Springs City for Champions initiative. | On-site shop: CC Tiger Store

name: Sertich Ice Center
address: 1705 E Pikes Peak Ave, Colorado Springs, CO 80909
price_public_skate: Child (17 & under): $6 | Adult (18+): $7 | Skate rental: $4 | Group rate: $5/skater incl. rental (book ahead) | Heroes discount $8 (military/first responders) | Check coloradosprings.gove Jan 2025)
notes: 35,000 sq ft facility built 1975 (renamed Sertich 1986). NHL regulation ice. 2,000-seat bleachers. Home to Pikes Peak Miners youth hockey. City of Colorado Springs Parks & Recreation. Email: sertichinfo@coloradosprings.gov. Price increases effective Jan 1, 2025.

name: Air Force Academy - Cadet Ice Arena

price_public_skate: N/A - limited public access
notes: Home of the Air Force Falcons. Primarily used for AFA athletics. Limited public access - call ahead. | On-site shop: USAFA Pro Shop

name: Monument Ice Rinks

notes: Two NHL-sized sheets between Colorado Springs and Denver. Strong youth and adult hockey programs. | On-site shop: Monument Pro Shop

Year-round hockey, figure skating, broomball. Home to Vail Junior Hockey Club and Skating Club of Vail. While closed, Vail Mountaineers Hockey Club organizing ice time at alternative venues. | On-site shop: Dobson Skate Shop

name: The Edge Ice Arena

notes: Part of Foothills Park and Recreation District. Two NHL-sized indoor rinks, 1250 total seating. Penalty Box sports bar & grill on main level. Broomball available (equipment provided). Contact: ahines@fhprd.org | On-site shop: Edge Pro Shop

Strong adult league scene — one of the friendliest drop-in crowds in the Denver metro per community reviews. Website may be intermittent — call (303) 986-4119 to confirm schedule. | On-site shop: Foothills Skate Corner

name: Ice Ranch
parking: Free surface lot. Handicap parking available. Elevator to upper level.

Home to Littleton Hawks, Regis Jesuit, Mountain Vista HS, Valor Christian HS, Cherry Creek. Also hosts 3v3 tournaments and adult leagues. | On-site shop: Precision Edge Hockey Pro Shop

name: South Suburban Sports Complex

notes: Replaced 52-year-old South Suburban Ice Arena. Three sheets: center sheet seats 800, second sheet 220, third sheet also available. Restaurant/bar with ice views. Drop-in hockey (adult & youth) $10.50 res/$11.50 non-res.

name: Apex Center
price_public_skate: Ages 2-5: Res. $2.50/Non-res. $3 | Ages 6-61: Res. $8/Non-res. $11.75 | Ages 62+: Res. $5.50/Non-res. $7.75
spectator_notes: Vending Machines: Vending machines only — no staffed food counter on-site. Plan ahead or grab food nearby.
notes: Part of Apex Park and Recreation District. Two NHL-sized ice surfaces. Figure skating program director Denise Hughes (303.463.4287). Adult Skaters Coffee Club Wednesdays 10:30am-12pm for $5.

Public skate sessions: $20/session (1-hour timed sessions), $5 skate rentals. Evening sessions Fri/Sat at 7pm and 9pm. Sessions are booked in advance at bigbearicearena.ezfacility.com - check calendar. Call (303) 343-1111 or email info@bigbearice.com. | On-site shop: Big Bear Skate Shop

name: University of Denver - Magness Arena
price_public_skate: N/A

Home to RoughRiders Sports Club (hockey, baseball, basketball, softball, volleyball). Located in Downtown Superior off US-36. | On-site shop: Blue Sport Stable Pro Shop

name: Blue Arena

notes: Part of Fort Collins' EPIC recreation complex with two NHL ice sheets plus pools. Home to CSU Hockey, Water Polo, Fort Collins Figure Skating Club, and FAST swim team. Over 1 million visits per year.

name: Lewis Ice Arena
price_public_skate: Adults: $10.50 | Youth/Child: $7 | Skate rental from $3 | Check aspenrecreation.com — Lewis open year-round
notes: NHL regulation 85x200 ft rink. 450-seat grandstand. Full skate shop with rentals and precision sharpening. ADA accessible. Multiple locker rooms with showers. name: Glenwood Springs Community Center Rink

notes: 85x200 ft covered outdoor NHL-sized rink. Seasonal Nov through mid-March. Part of Glenwood Springs Community Center (65,000 sq ft facility with pool, gym, climbing wall, fitness center). Call ice rink direct at (970) 384-6304, main center (970) 384-6300.

name: River City Sportplex
price_public_skate: Check rivercitysportplex.com for current rates

Party room packages available for 10-40 guests. | On-site shop: River City Pro Shop

notes: City of Steamboat Springs-operated Olympic-sized indoor ice rink open July through May. Colorado's only rink with Bumper Cars On Ice ($12/ride or $19 combo with skating + rental). Public skate, stick & puck, drop-in hockey, figure skating classes, hockey leagues, speed skating, curling, broomball. Home to Steamboat Springs Youth Hockey Association, Sailors HS Hockey Team, and Steamboat Springs Figure Skating Club. Check Public Google Calendar on city website for daily schedule. | On-site shop: Howelsen Skate Shop

name: Ice Centre at The Promenade
price_public_skate: $10 adults | Kids 3 & under free with paying adult
spectator_notes: Bender's Bar & Grill: Full sports bar & restaurant with ice views. Red City Café also on-site for coffee & smoothies.
notes: 143,000 sq ft, $14M facility. One of few triple-sheet facilities in North America. Total seating 1,600. Bender's Bar & Grill on-site. Home to Hyland Hills Jaguars and Rocky Mountain Figure Skating Club. Westminster/Hyland Hills discount cards accepted. | On-site shop: Ice Centre Pro Shop, Bender's Bar & Grill

name: The Ice Box (Fraser Valley Sports Complex)
parking: Free lot at Fraser Valley Sports Complex
price_public_skate: $13/person (includes skate rental) | Check fraservalleyrec.org for current rates
notes: NHL-sized partially enclosed/covered outdoor refrigerated rink at the Fraser Valley Sports Complex. Managed by Fraser Valley Metropolitan Recreation District. Features skate rentals, warming house, indoor concessions, restrooms. Strong youth hockey leagues. Fraser is known as the 'Icebox of the Nation' for its extreme cold. Also has ball fields, volleyball, soccer. Call ahead for open skating times as they are limited.

name: Jorgensen Ice Rink & Events Center
price_public_skate: Check gunnisonco.gov for 2025-26 season rates
notes: Gunnison Valley's premier winter venue. Includes indoor ice facility (open late Sep–Mar), outdoor ice rink, and free Pac Man Pond for public skating daily. Also features Nordic trails and jib park. Home to West Elk Hockey Association. Indoor rink manager: Andy Eflin (970) 641-0435. Full gear recommended for open hockey; helmet & gloves required for stick & puck. | On-site shop: Concessions area

name: Pueblo Plaza Ice Arena
price_public_skate: Check puebloicearena.com for current rates
notes: Year-round Southern Colorado hub. Home to Pueblo Bulls Youth Hockey (PBYHA), Pueblo County HS, Pueblo Figure Skating Club, Men's/Women's/Co-ed leagues, and Pueblo Bulls NCDC junior team. Skating Academy offers 8-week sessions. Laser light skate, holiday skates, and college nights hosted regularly. Contact: 719-621-9191 or puebloicearena1@gmail.com. | On-site shop: Pueblo Plaza Shop

name: Alamosa Ice Arena
price_public_skate: Check website for current rates (passes available Oct 1)
notes: Outdoor partially covered ice rink at the Alamosa Family Recreation Center. Seasonal (Thanksgiving–Feb, weather permitting). Hockey leagues hosted here. Skate rentals available. All skating first come first served - not reservable. Ice rink passes available Oct 1. For info contact Alamosa Family Recreation Center.

name: Loudy-Simpson Park Ice Arena
price_public_skate: $6/person per session | $3 skate rental | ⚠️ CASH OR CHECK ONLY
notes: Moffat County facility, managed by Colorado Extreme Hockey Association since September 2024. 85x125 ft ice surface (not full NHL size). Seasonal Oct–mid-March. CASH OR CHECK ONLY — no credit cards accepted. 4 dressing rooms, bleachers. Public skating, stick & puck (drop-in hockey), Craig Youth Hockey League, and curling on Fridays.

name: Evergreen Lake Ice Rinks
address: 29612 Upper Bear Creek Rd, Evergreen, CO 80439
parking: Limited - weekends use Stagecoach Park shuttle
price_public_skate: $15/person all day
notes: World's largest Zamboni-maintained outdoor natural ice rink. 8.5 acres with 11 pond hockey rinks. Seasonal - weather dependent. | On-site shop: Evergreen Lake House

name: Keystone Lake Ice Rink
price_public_skate: Adults: $15 | Kids (5-17): $10 | Ages 4 & under: $5 | Hours: 10am-9pm daily | Check keystoneresort.com for current rates
price_stick_puck: Hockey box available on lake - free with admission
notes: 5-acre outdoor lake rink at Lakeside Village. Largest Zamboni-maintained outdoor rink in North America (Evergreen Lake is larger at 8.5 acres but Keystone claims largest for a resort). Hours: Daily 10am-9pm. Adjacent Adventure Center has full bar, snacks, indoor arcade. Skate assists, hockey sticks and child pull sleds available to rent. Hockey box available. Two rinks at Keystone: Lake Rink (Lakeside Village) and Dercum Square Ice Rink (River Run Village - smaller, free admission). Note: admission to Lake Rink includes all-day access.

name: Dillon Marina Outdoor Rink
price_public_skate: FREE - open sunrise to sunset
price_stick_puck: Free drop-in hockey on the rink
notes: FREE community ice rink on the Dillon Reservoir at Dillon Marina. Open sunrise to sundown. 136 ft x 70 ft natural ice with stunning views of Lake Dillon and Tenmile/Gore Ranges. Night lighting until 9pm. NO skate rentals on-site - bring your own. Drop-in hockey and broomball organized seasonally. NOTE: The Lake Loops trails on the reservoir did not open in 2025-26 season due to late freeze. Check townofdillon.com for current season status.

name: Eagle Ice Rink

notes: Outdoor seasonal rink in Eagle serving the Eagle Valley community. Between Vail and Glenwood Springs on I-70.

name: Big Mine Ice Arena

name: NedRINK

notes: Olympic-sized outdoor ice rink at Nederland Ice & Racquet Park - claims to be Colorado's largest outdoor ice rink. Non-profit organization staffed by volunteers, established 2005. Open daily Dec-Feb, weather permitting (check webcam on website). Programs: recreational skating, hockey leagues, curling, stick & puck, broomball, group/private lessons ($15/hr). Daily pass: $12 (Ned & Gilpin County residents: $10). Seasonal passes available. Near Frozen Dead Guy Days festival in March. Phone: 720-514-9624.

name: Oak Creek Ice Rink

Programs include youth/adult hockey leagues, curling, women's and co-ed drop-in hockey, figure skating lessons. Address is also listed as 505 W 5th St. Season: Dec-Feb (weather permitting). Check lakecountyco.gov for current schedule.

name: Winter Park Village Pond
parking: Free small gondola lot; main resort parking garage also available
price_public_skate: FREE with own skates | Skate rental ~$19/hr at Adventure & Supply Co.
notes: Picturesque outdoor pond rink in the heart of Winter Park Resort's alpine village. FREE admission with your own skates. Skate rentals available at Adventure & Supply Co. (~$19/hr). Glow Night Cosmic Skating every Thu/Fri/Sat 5-7pm with DJ and colorful lights. Open mid-November to mid-April. Views of the Continental Divide. Ice maintained daily by resort. | On-site shop: Adventure & Supply Co.

name: Edwards Freedom Park Outdoor Ice Rinks
price_public_skate: FREE — no admission charge
price_stick_puck: FREE — drop-in on the hockey rink
notes: Community outdoor ice rinks at Edwards Freedom Park, managed by Mountain Recreation District with dedicated volunteer Tom Boyd. Now in their 4th+ season. Two rinks: a larger hockey rink and a smaller general skating rink, both built on turf using a specialized board/liner system. FREE to skate. Loaner skates available in storage boxes near baseball dugouts (first-come, limited supply). Golf-cart Zamboni maintained by volunteers. Part of a beloved neighborhood tradition — kids skate after school, adults at lunch. Contact Mountain Rec at (970) 766-5555.

name: Montrose Rotary Park Ice Rink
address: Rotary Park, 1020 S Townsend Ave, Montrose, CO 81401
price_public_skate: FREE
notes: Natural non-refrigerated ice pond at Rotary Park in Montrose. City of Montrose Parks & Cemeteries maintains the rink when conditions allow — typically 1–6 weeks per winter depending on weather. 100x200 ft cleared area. FREE admission, NO skate rentals on-site. Ice safety verified by city staff (3-inch drill test). Locals drop in for hockey and broomball. Skate at your own risk per posted signage. Contact City of Montrose Parks at (970) 249-7505.

name: Pagosa Multi-Purpose Pavilion Ice Rink
price_public_skate: Check skatepagosa.org for current season rates | Season passes & punch cards available
notes: Pagosa Springs' community rink at South Town Park Pavilion. Outdoor seasonal rink with a full program: public skating, drop-in hockey (Saturdays), broomball (Sundays), curling with Riff Raff Brewing (Tuesdays), and figure skating. Skate rentals available. Season passes and punch cards available. Private lessons available — call (970) 398-2337. Email: skatepagosa@icloud.com. Pair with a soak at The Springs Resort natural hot spring complex nearby — a perfect post-skate tradition. Also note: free natural ice skating on Town ponds behind River Center when thick enough (4+ inches), no fee, no rentals.
